title: MailerLite vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare MailerLite vs Drip. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool wins for your business needs.
intro: MailerLite and Drip are both powerful email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. MailerLite excels as an affordable, beginner-friendly option with a generous free tier, while Drip is purpose-built for e-commerce businesses seeking advanced revenue tracking and integration. Choose based on your budget, business type, and required features.

comparison_table.tool_a: Drag-drop editor, landing pages, pop-ups, surveys, e-commerce integrations
comparison_table.tool_b: E-commerce tracking, revenue attribution, visual automation, SMS, onsite campaigns

choose_a_if: You're starting out and want a free email marketing solution with no credit card required
You need affordable pricing with landing pages, pop-ups, and basic e-commerce features
You prefer an intuitive drag-drop editor and want to avoid steep learning curves
choose_b_if: Your primary focus is e-commerce with advanced revenue tracking and attribution needs
You need SMS marketing alongside email and onsite campaign capabilities
You're willing to invest in a premium solution built specifically for online stores

faq.a: It depends on your needs. MailerLite is better for budget-conscious startups and small businesses due to its free tier and lower pricing. Drip is better for established e-commerce businesses needing advanced revenue tracking and store integration. MailerLite scores higher overall for versatility, while Drip excels specifically for online sellers.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, MailerLite or Drip?
faq.a: MailerLite is significantly cheaper, offering a free plan for up to 1,000 subscribers and paid plans starting at $9/month. Drip has no free tier and starts at $39/month. For budget-conscious teams, MailerLite's freemium model provides excellent value.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to MailerLite?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support subscriber list exports and integrations with common email marketing tools. Most migrations involve exporting contacts and setting up automation rules in MailerLite. Both tools have support resources to help with the transition process.
